Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Q5 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

As per IS 456 :2000 the maximum strain in tension reinforcement for Fe500 grade of steel shall not be less than ________× 10–3. 

(Correct upto three decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 4.175
Solution

4.175 (4.170 - 4.180)

Q7 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A rectangular reinforced concrete beam of width 350 mm and overall depth 750 mm is subjected to ultimate hogging bending moment of 200 kN-m and ultimate twisting moment of 140 kN-m. The equivalent bending moment (Me) for the design of longitudinal tensile reinforcement at top (in kN-m) is _______. (Correct upto two decimal places).

NAT
Correct answer: 458.82
Solution

458.82 (457 - 460)

 
Q8 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

The effective modulus of elasticity of a particular concrete is 11377 MPa. The concrete was loaded after 28 days from casting. For this concrete, the target mean strength for design mix (in MPa) shall be ______ . (Correct upto two decimal places).

NAT
Correct answer: 43.25
Solution

43.25 (42.75 - 43.75)

Correct answer: B

Q11 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A beam with four 32 mm bars as main tension steel has two of its bars symmetrically bent a the ends of the beam at 45°. The design shear force to be resisted by the stirrups, if the factored shear force at critical section is 420 kN, is _________ kN.
Assume, b = 350 mm, d = 550 mm, fck = 25 N/mm2 and fy = 415 N/mm2. τc,max = 3.1 N/mm2, τc = 0.6 N/mm2

NAT
Correct answer: 152.075
Solution

152.075 (150 - 154)

 
Q12 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following option(s) is/are correct regarding assumptions made in working stress method?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, c, d)
All tensile stresses are assumed to be taken by the reinforcement alone and none by the concrete.
This means that while calculating the moment of resistance of the beam, the contribution of the
concrete below neutral axis is to be completely neglected.

A reinforced concrete beam having width 300 mm and effective depth 500 mm is reinforced with 20 mm diameter bars of Fe415 steel. The nominal cover to the reinforcement is 25 mm and diameter of stirrups is 8 mm. As per the provisions of IS456:2000 which of the following options is/are correct?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(b, d)

 
Q16 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A doubly reinforced concrete beam of size 300 mm × 550 mm overall depth is reinforced with 2- 12 mm diameter bars in compression zone and 4-20 mm diameter bars in tension zone, each at an effective cover of 40 mm. If M25 grade of concrete and Fe415 grade of steel are used, then which of the following options is/are correct? [Assume stress in compression steel as 350 MPa.]

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b)

Correct answer: A

(a)
